story: In King Matthias’s time, a rich brother gave his poor sister his worst cow, expecting it to die. She nursed it back to health, and it calved a fine little bull. The brother claimed the calf, saying he had given her only a cow. They argued before a judge, who delayed so long that the calf grew into a great bull. Their quarrel reached King Matthias. When he came to the village and saw the animal, he said he wanted to buy it. The judge pointed to the arguing brother and sister. King Matthias stopped them and decided to settle the matter with three questions. As he listened, the king privately resolved that he would award the bull to the person whose answers showed real wisdom, not to the one who praised him most.
implausible_knowledge: the king privately resolved that he would award the bull to the person whose answers showed real wisdom, not to the one who praised him most
question: When King Matthias asks the three questions, what is the rich brother most likely to do to try to win the bull?
answer_with_knowledge: Try to give thoughtful, philosophical answers about things like thought, the earth, and the sun.
answer_without_knowledge: Give flattering, conventional answers that praise his own stallion, the king, and money.
